question: What type of steam generator does NuScale use?

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The electrically-heated steam generator tests at the SIET facility used instrumented tubes with controlled boundary conditions to simulate different heat flux zones for subcooled, boiling, and superheated regions, enabling precise measurement and analysis. The data collected supported evaluation of pressure fluctuations, informed the design of flow restrictors, validated thermal-hydraulic models, and defined steam outlet conditions, with oversight from the NRC to ensure compliance with safety procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0451.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsPSGD B.1 thru B.4Preheat Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0451.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, and therefore the regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in such systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently precluding water hammer concerns addressed by the cited guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuScale NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The testing aimed to gather CHF data, validate and develop CHF correlations, and measure thermal-hydraulic characteristics using an electrically heated 5x5 simulated fuel bundle at the AREVA Karlstein facility. The results support the safety analysis and design validation for NuScale’s reactor application.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing standard symbols and abbreviations for valve positions, actuator types, and control elements. It explains the normal operating status of valves, exceptions, and the meaning of various actuator and valve status codes to ensure consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0452.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.1 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Auxiliary Feedwater Supply
nuscale_ch01_0452.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4, and therefore the associated water hammer guidelines are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently avoiding the water hammer issues targeted by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0003.title: Plant Location
nuscale_ch01_0003.summary: The section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It provides an overview of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), a modular, scalable nuclear steam supply system, and highlights key design features such as passive safety systems, compact containment, and advanced digital controls. The FSAR covers both single-module and multi-module (up to 12) plant configurations.

nuscale_ch01_0773.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) First sentence of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Methodology and Assumptions for Calculating Radiological Consequences
nuscale_ch01_0773.summary: The section discusses the methodology and assumptions for calculating radiological consequences of a steam generator tube failure in a pressurized water reactor (PWR). It states that while the guidance for calculating these consequences is applicable to the NuScale Design Certification Application (DCA), the specific radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by those in SRP Section 15.0.3, as referenced in the review interfaces. Thus, only the calculation methodology is followed, not the acceptance criteria from this section.

nuscale_ch01_0060.title: NuScale Integral System Test Program
nuscale_ch01_0060.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program was established to collect thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and to validate safety analysis codes using a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design. The NIST-1 facility replicates key reactor systems and supports the development and validation of proprietary safety codes, ensuring accurate modeling of the NPM’s unique features. Testing at NIST-1 has provided essential data for NuScale’s safety analysis and design certification efforts.

nuscale_ch01_0114.title: Figure 1.7-3c: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0114.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various equipment such as tanks, vessels, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, filters, and HVAC components. It serves as a reference to interpret the different types of process and mechanical equipment shown in the facility's engineering drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0772.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) First paragraph and Items (1) and (2) of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Acceptability of Site and Dose Mitigating ESF Systems
nuscale_ch01_0772.summary: The guidance in SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2 regarding the calculation of radiological consequences from a postulated steam generator tube failure is applicable to the NuScale Design Certification Application (DCA). However, the specific radiological acceptance criteria referenced in this section are superseded by those in SRP Section 15.0.3, meaning only the calculation methodology is followed, not the acceptance limits.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer fuel assemblies, and simplified systems, such as having no external coolant loops or reactor coolant pumps. The comparison is intended to illustrate the unique aspects and safety features of the NuScale design relative to conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0453.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.2 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Tests and Test Procedures
nuscale_ch01_0453.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4 guidelines for avoiding water hammers. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that inherently avoids the water hammer concerns targeted by these guidelines, making the specific regulatory criteria not applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch01_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifying that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document as needed for regulatory purposes. It also notes that proprietary information is protected, and any copies made must include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

edges.description: Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic testing of steam generator tubes using electrically-heated methods, while Section B references regulatory guidelines for preheat steam generator designs and clarifies that NuScale's design fulfills similar functions to traditional systems but with different implementation, connecting them through the type and function of the steam generator used in NuScale's design.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the steam generator using electrically-heated tubes, while Section B details critical heat flux testing for the NuScale fuel design using a similar electrically-heated facility, indicating both sections discuss different aspects of testing using electrically-heated setups relevant to NuScale's steam generator and fuel design.

Section B references 'Once-Through Steam Generator Designs' as relevant to NuScale, while Section A describes testing of steam generator tubes consistent with once-through designs, thus Section A provides supporting technical detail for the design type referenced in Section B.

Section A provides specific technical details about steam generator testing methods, while Section B gives a general overview of the NuScale plant and its documentation, with Section A specifying details for the broader context described in Section B.

Section A discusses design guidelines and regulatory conformance for steam generators, while Section B details thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the NuScale steam generator, with both sections connected by their focus on the technical and regulatory aspects of NuScale's steam generator design.

Section A discusses regulatory guidelines and design conformance for steam generators, while Section B provides detailed P&ID legends used in NuScale's safety analysis report, with both sections referencing documentation related to NuScale's steam generator systems.

Section A discusses preheat steam generator designs while Section B discusses once-through steam generator designs, both in the context of NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria, indicating that these are alternative steam generator types considered for NuScale.

Section A discusses specific design guidelines and regulatory conformance related to steam generators in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B introduces the FSAR document that contains general descriptions of the plant, including the steam generator type, thus Section B provides the broader context for the specific details in Section A.

Section B references the use of once-through steam generator designs in the context of regulatory guidelines, which implies that NuScale uses a once-through steam generator, thus providing information that answers the analysis question posed in Section A.

Section A provides specific technical testing details related to the NuScale fuel and steam generator performance, while Section B offers a general overview of the NuScale plant design, with Section A specifying details for the broader descriptions in Section B.

Section B discusses design guidelines for once-through steam generator designs and explicitly references the NuScale design in the context of regulatory conformance, while Section A provides P&ID legends but does not specify the steam generator type; thus, Section B provides the relevant information that Section A lacks.

Section A discusses specific design guidelines and regulatory conformance related to steam generator types and auxiliary feedwater systems, while Section B provides the general introduction and scope of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, which includes descriptions of plant systems such as the steam generator; thus, Section A specifies details for the general descriptions found in Section B.

Section B describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing methodology for NuScale's steam generator tubes, providing technical details that support the information in Section A regarding NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for steam generator tube failure analysis.

Section B discusses design guidelines for preheat steam generator designs and references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, which is also cited in Section A as part of its conformance with regulatory criteria, thus both sections are connected through their reliance on the NuScale FSAR to address regulatory requirements for steam generator design and performance.

Section B provides technical testing data and design details (e.g., CHF testing for the steam generator and fuel design) that support the regulatory conformance discussions in Section A regarding NuScale's steam generator performance and safety analysis.

Section A describes the overall NuScale integral system test program, which includes system-level thermal-hydraulic testing, while Section B details a specific steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance test that provides data relevant to the steam generator's design and operation as referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the testing and validation of the NuScale reactor's thermal-hydraulic systems, while Section B discusses regulatory guidelines for steam generator designs and clarifies that certain guidelines for large PWRs do not apply to NuScale due to its different design (specifically, the use of DHRS instead of AFW), thus Section B provides regulatory context for interpreting the technical information in Section A.

Section A describes the integral system test program for the overall NuScale reactor system, while Section B details specific thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the steam generator using an electrically heated facility, with both sections connected by their focus on generating thermal-hydraulic data to validate and inform the NuScale reactor's design and safety analysis.

Section A provides diagram legends for various components, including vessels and pumps, but does not specify the type of steam generator, while Section B discusses regulatory guidelines for steam generator design and clarifies that certain guidelines for large PWRs do not apply to NuScale, implying a difference in steam generator design.

Section B provides technical details about the steam generator tube testing method (electrically-heated, fluid-heated facility) that supports the regulatory conformance discussion in Section A regarding NuScale's steam generator design.

Section B discusses design guidelines for preheat steam generator designs and references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, which is also cited in Section A as part of its conformance with regulatory criteria, thus both sections are connected through their reliance on the NuScale FSAR to address regulatory requirements for steam generator design and safety analysis.

Section B provides technical testing data on the thermal-hydraulic performance of the NuScale steam generator, which supports the regulatory conformance discussions in Section A regarding steam generator tube failure analysis.

Section B describes the NuScale Integral System Test Program, which provides technical data and validation for the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design referenced in Section A's regulatory conformance discussion, thereby supporting the regulatory analysis in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance and methodology for analyzing steam generator tube failures in NuScale's safety analysis report, while Section B provides diagram legends for equipment types (including vessels) used in NuScale's system drawings, thus Section B could provide visual or technical context for the steam generator type referenced in Section A.

Section A references the methodology and assumptions for calculating radiological consequences of steam generator tube failure, while Section B provides the regulatory criteria and acceptability of site and dose mitigating ESF systems for such failures, thus Section A's analysis is based on the requirements and criteria specified in Section B.

Section A describes the NuScale integral system test program, which includes testing of the reactor coolant system and steam generator, while Section B discusses regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of steam generator tube failure, connecting them through the need for test data (from A) to demonstrate conformance with regulatory requirements (in B).

Section A provides a general comparison of NuScale's design features, including steam generator parameters, while Section B details the specific thermal-hydraulic performance testing conducted on the steam generator tubes, thus Section B provides supporting technical evidence for the design features summarized in Section A.

Section A provides a comparison of NuScale's steam generator design features and parameters with those of a typical PWR, while Section B discusses regulatory guidelines for steam generator designs and clarifies that certain guidelines for large PWRs do not apply to NuScale due to differences in system design.

Section A provides a general comparison of NuScale's design features, including steam generator type, while Section B details thermal-hydraulic testing related to the steam generator's fuel design, thus Section B provides supporting technical evidence for the design features summarized in Section A.

Section B describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing conducted on isolated tubes representative of a once-through steam generator, which is referenced in Section A as the design type used by NuScale.

Section A discusses once-through steam generator designs and their associated tests, while Section B discusses preheat steam generator designs, both in the context of NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria, thus contrasting two different steam generator types relevant to the analysis question.

Section A references design guidelines and regulatory conformance for once-through steam generator designs, while Section B describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing conducted on the NuScale steam generator, both connecting through the focus on the once-through steam generator type used by NuScale.

Section B provides technical details about the steam generator tube testing methodology, which supports the general plant description referenced in Section A, thereby offering evidence relevant to identifying the type of steam generator used by NuScale.

Section B discusses design guidelines for steam generators and references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, while Section A is the preface to the NuScale Design Certification Application, indicating that Section B's technical content is part of the detailed documentation introduced in Section A.

Section B discusses thermal-hydraulic performance testing and critical heat flux data for the NuScale steam generator and fuel design, providing technical details that are referenced or elaborated upon in the general plant description found in Section A.

Section A references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and its conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B is the preface to the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, which includes the referenced safety analysis report, thus Section A cites Section B as a source document.
